name: Australian Vegemite Toast
steps: Toast the bread until golden.
Spread with the butter, then a very thin layer of Vegemite - it's meant to be a whisper, not a slab.
Serve immediately while warm.
steps_detailed: Toast the bread until golden and crisp.
Spread generously with the butter first, all the way to the edges.
Spread a very thin, even layer of Vegemite over the butter - the classic mistake is using too much, since it's intensely salty and savory.
Serve immediately while the toast is still warm.
description: Vegemite, a salty, savory yeast-extract spread, is a genuine Australian icon - used sparingly, since its flavor is intensely concentrated compared to how thickly other spreads are typically applied.

name: Australian Bacon and Egg Roll
steps: Fry the bacon until crisp, then fry the egg in the same pan.
Warm the roll and split it.
Build the sandwich with the bacon, egg, and a swipe of tomato sauce.
steps_detailed: Fry the bacon in a skillet over medium heat until crisp, 4-5 minutes, then set aside.
Fry the eggs in the same pan (the bacon fat adds flavor) to your preferred doneness.
Warm and split the bread roll, buttering the inside.
Layer the bacon and eggs into the roll and finish with a generous swipe of tomato sauce - an Australian weekend breakfast staple, ideally from a bakery.
description: A simple bacon and egg roll, an Australian breakfast staple, especially from bakeries and cafes.

name: Australian Avocado Toast
steps: Toast the bread until golden.
Mash the avocado with the lime juice and pepper.
Spread generously over the toast.
steps_detailed: Toast the bread until golden and crisp.
Mash the avocado with the lime juice, black pepper, and jalapeno if using, keeping some texture.
Spread generously over the toast and top with the crumbled feta - this dish is credited to Australian and New Zealand cafes before it spread worldwide.
description: Avocado toast, popularized in Australian cafes before spreading globally, is now closely associated with Australian brunch culture.

name: Australian Meat Pie
steps: Brown the beef and simmer with the broth until thickened into a rich filling, about 10-12 minutes.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, and cold water.
Fill the pastry, top with more pastry, and bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Brown the ground beef with the onion, then stir in the tomato paste and beef broth.
Whisk the cornstarch with a splash of water and stir in, simmering 8-10 minutes until the filling is thick and gravy-like. Let cool slightly.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, a pinch of salt, and cold water, rolling into two rounds.
Line a small pie dish with one round, fill with the meat mixture, and top with the second round, crimping the edges and cutting a vent.
description: Brush with the egg wash and bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until deeply golden - the ultimate Australian handheld lunch, traditionally topped with tomato sauce.

name: Australian Chicken Parmigiana
steps: Bread the chicken (flour, then egg) and fry until golden and cooked through.
Top with the diced tomatoes and shredded mozzarella.
Broil briefly until the cheese melts and bubbles.
steps_detailed: Pound the chicken to an even thickness and season with salt and pepper.
Dredge in flour, then egg, then the cornmeal, pressing to help it adhere.
Fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 3-4 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through.
Top with the diced tomatoes, oregano, and shredded mozzarella.
description: Broil 2-3 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling - known simply as a 'parma' or 'parmi' and a beloved pub lunch across the country, often with a side of chips.

name: Australian Burger with Beetroot
steps: Shape the beef into a patty, season, and grill or pan-fry to your liking.
Fry the egg.
Build the burger with the patty, beetroot slices, and fried egg - the distinctly Australian addition.
steps_detailed: Shape the beef into a patty and season well with salt and pepper.
Grill or pan-fry in the oil, 3-4 minutes per side, to your preferred doneness.
Fry the egg in the same pan to your liking.
Warm and split the bun, and build the burger with the patty, sliced beetroot, fried egg, cabbage, tomato, and a swipe of tomato sauce - beetroot on a burger is a proudly Australian tradition.
description: A beetroot slice on a burger is a distinctly Australian addition, rarely seen elsewhere.

name: Australian Fish and Chips
steps: Cut the potato into fries and fry until golden, about 10-12 minutes, then set aside.
Dust the fish in flour and fry until golden and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve together with a wedge of lime.
steps_detailed: Cut the potatoes into fries and fry in the oil over medium-high heat until golden and crisp, 10-14 minutes total, then drain and set aside.
Whisk the flour, baking powder, black pepper, and a splash of cold water into a thick, smooth batter.
Dip the fish in the batter and fry in the same oil, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through.
description: Serve the fish and chips together with a good squeeze of lime and, traditionally, plenty of tomato sauce - a beachside classic all along the Australian coast.

name: Australian BBQ Lamb Chops

ingredients.note: formed into chops, or use lamb chops if available

steps: Season the lamb well with the garlic, oregano, and a little oil.
Grill or pan-sear over high heat until well charred outside and cooked to your liking, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Rest a few minutes before serving.

ingredients_full.note: formed into chops, or use lamb chops if available

steps_detailed: Combine the garlic, oregano, oil, lime juice, and black pepper, and rub over the lamb. Marinate at least 20 minutes.
Grill over high heat (or sear in a very hot skillet), 3-5 minutes per side depending on thickness, until well charred outside.
Rest 5 minutes before serving - lamb on the barbie is an Australian weekend institution.
description: Grilled lamb chops, a staple of the classic Australian barbecue.

name: Australian Barramundi with Lemon
steps: Season the fish and sear in the butter, 3-4 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through.
Finish with a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve immediately.
steps_detailed: Season the fish with black pepper and sear in the butter with the garlic over medium-high heat, 4-5 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through.
Finish with a generous squeeze of lime juice, letting it bubble briefly in the hot pan.
Scatter with cilantro and serve immediately - barramundi is one of Australia's signature native fish, prized for its mild, buttery flavor.
description: Barramundi, a native Australian fish, prepared here simply with lemon.

name: Australian Chicken Schnitzel
steps: Bread the chicken (flour, then egg) and fry until golden and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve with a wedge of lime and a simple salad.
steps_detailed: Pound the chicken breast to an even thickness and season with salt and black pepper.
Dredge in flour, then egg, then the cornmeal, pressing gently to help it adhere.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and fry the chicken 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Serve with a squeeze of lime juice and a simple green salad - plain schnitzel is the base for a parma if you add tomato and cheese.
description: Schnitzel, brought by European immigrants, became a beloved Australian pub classic, especially topped with gravy and cheese as a 'parma'.

name: Australian Sausage Roll
steps: Mix the beef with the onion and season well, then shape into a log.
Wrap in a simple butter pastry made from the flour, butter, and cold water.
Bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until the pastry is golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the ground beef with the onion and tomato paste, seasoning well with salt and pepper, and shape into a log about 1.5 inches thick.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, a pinch of salt, and cold water, rolling into a rectangle.
Wrap the pastry around the meat log, sealing the seam and ends.
Brush with the egg wash and bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until deeply golden.
Slice and serve warm with tomato sauce - a footy-match and school-lunch staple.
description: The sausage roll, flaky pastry around seasoned sausage meat, is an Australian bakery and party staple.

name: Australian Fairy Bread
steps: Spread the bread generously with butter, all the way to the edges.
Scatter with the sugar (traditionally rainbow sprinkles, known as 'hundreds and thousands').
Cut into triangles and serve.
steps_detailed: Use soft white bread and spread it generously with butter all the way to the edges, so the sprinkles have something to stick to.
Scatter the sugar (or rainbow sprinkles, known locally as 'hundreds and thousands') evenly over the buttered bread, pressing gently so they adhere.
description: Cut into triangles and serve immediately - a beloved Australian kids' birthday party staple, simple and a little nostalgic.

name: Australian Anzac-Style Savory Damper
steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and rub in the butter until crumbly.
Add the milk and bring together into a soft dough.
Shape into a round and bake at 400F for 20-25 minutes until golden and it sounds hollow when tapped.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, black pepper, and a pinch of salt in a bowl, then rub in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the milk gradually, bringing the mixture together into a soft, slightly sticky dough - don't overwork it.
Shape into a round loaf and score a cross on top.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 22-28 minutes until golden brown and it sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom.
Damper is traditional Australian bush bread, cooked over campfires by stockmen and swagmen - this is an oven-friendly version.
description: Damper, a simple soda bread traditionally cooked over an open fire, was a staple for early settlers and shearers.

name: Australian Chiko Roll
steps: Brown the beef, then mix with the finely shredded cabbage and carrot.
Roll the filling tightly in the tortilla, sealing the edge.
Fry until golden and crisp on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the ground beef with the onion, then stir in the cabbage, carrot, and soy sauce, cooking 4-5 minutes until the vegetables soften slightly but keep some crunch. Let cool.
Make a flour-water paste to help seal the edge of the wrapper.
Spoon the cooled filling onto the wrapper and roll tightly into a cylinder, sealing the edge with the flour paste.
Heat the oil in a skillet and fry the roll, turning occasionally, 5-7 minutes until deeply golden and crisp on all sides.
A servo (gas station) and footy-match staple, the Chiko Roll is a distinctly Australian invention.
description: The Chiko roll, a large fried spring-roll-style snack invented in Australia in the 1950s, remains a beloved takeaway food, especially at football matches.

name: Australian Lamingtons
steps: Beat the egg and sugar until pale, then fold in the flour to make a simple sponge batter.
Bake at 350F for 18-20 minutes until springy, then cool and cut into squares.
Mix the cocoa with a little water and sugar into a thin glaze, dip each square, then roll in the coconut.
steps_detailed: Beat the eggs and sugar together until pale and thickened, then fold in the melted butter.
Sift the flour and baking powder together and fold in gently until just combined.
Pour into a small buttered baking dish and bake at 350F (175C) for 18-22 minutes until springy and golden. Let cool completely, then cut into small squares.
Whisk the cocoa powder with a little hot water and a spoonful of sugar into a thin, pourable chocolate glaze.
Dip each sponge square into the glaze, letting the excess drip off, then roll immediately in the shredded coconut.
description: Let set on a rack before serving - a beloved Australian classic, named for a former state governor.

name: Australian Pavlova

ingredients.note: tbsp, whipped, as a cream stand-in

ingredients.ingredient: Pineapple, canned, juice pack, solids and liquids

steps: Whip the egg whites until foamy, then gradually add the sugar and whip until thick, glossy, and holding stiff peaks.
Spread into a round on a lined baking sheet and bake at 250F for 50-60 minutes until crisp outside, then turn off the oven and let cool inside.
Top with the whipped cream and fruit before serving.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, whipped with a little sugar

ingredients_full.ingredient: Pineapple, canned, juice pack, solids and liquids

steps_detailed: Whip the egg whites until foamy, then gradually add the sugar a spoonful at a time, whipping continuously until thick, glossy, and holding stiff, shiny peaks.
Gently fold in the cornstarch and vinegar, which help give the classic marshmallowy center.
Spread the meringue into a round mound on a parchment-lined baking sheet, smoothing the sides.
Bake at 250F (120C) for 55-65 minutes until the outside is crisp and pale, then turn off the oven and let it cool completely inside with the door slightly ajar, to prevent cracking.
description: Top with the whipped cream and fruit just before serving - Australia and New Zealand have a long-running friendly dispute over who invented this dessert.

name: Australian Anzac Biscuits

ingredients.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Melt the butter with the syrup.
Mix the oats, flour, and coconut, then stir in the melted butter mixture until combined.
Shape into small rounds and bake at 325F for 15-18 minutes until golden.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Melt the butter with the maple syrup (standing in for the traditional golden syrup) over low heat.
Mix the oats, flour, coconut, brown sugar, and baking powder in a bowl.
Pour the melted butter mixture over the dry ingredients and stir until well combined into a firm dough.
Shape into small rounds, flatten slightly, and place on a baking sheet.
description: Bake at 325F (165C) for 15-20 minutes until golden brown - Anzac biscuits were sent to soldiers abroad since the ingredients kept well without refrigeration, and they remain a beloved tradition around Anzac Day.

name: Australian BBQ Sauce
steps: Whisk the tomato paste, maple syrup, and vinegar together with a splash of water until smooth.
Simmer 5 minutes to meld the flavors.
Use as a glaze or dipping sauce - Australian BBQ sauce tends to be sweeter and tangier than American versions.
steps_detailed: Combine the tomato paste, maple syrup, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, and garlic with 2 tablespoons water in a small saucepan.
Simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 6-8 minutes until slightly thickened and the flavors have melded.
Let cool before using as a glaze for grilled meats or a dipping sauce for pies and sausage rolls.
description: A simple barbecue sauce, common at Australian cookouts.

name: Australian Beetroot Relish
steps: Combine the chopped beets with the vinegar and sugar.
Simmer 8-10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Serve alongside burgers or sandwiches.
steps_detailed: Combine the finely chopped beets, vinegar, sugar, and onion in a small saucepan.
Simmer over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until thickened to a spoonable relish.
Let cool before serving alongside burgers, sandwiches, or grilled meats.
description: Beetroot, closely associated with Australian burgers and sandwiches, made here into a simple relish.

name: Australian Gravy
steps: Melt the butter and whisk in the flour, cooking 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, and simmer 4-5 minutes until thickened.
Serve over a meat pie or roast.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the flour, and cook, stirring, 1-2 minutes until lightly golden.
Gradually whisk in the beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, working out any lumps.
Simmer 5-6 minutes, whisking occasionally, until thickened to a rich, pourable gravy.
Season with black pepper and pour generously over a meat pie, mash, or a Sunday roast.
description: A simple gravy, common alongside meat pies and roasts.
